text: Science has a clearer story about induction motors than social media does. Induction motors are everywhere—from your fridge to factory machines—but how do they actually work? They use electromagnetic fields to create motion without direct contact. Fascinating, right? In simple terms, a rotating magnetic field induces a current in the rotor, turning it. It’s efficient and reliable. And here’s the twist: while they’re efficient, induction motors can still waste energy as heat. A fine balance! Curious about more tech mysteries? Like and follow for deeper dives into the science that powers our world!
